mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 tyrosine kinase, blocking FLT3‑driven signaling and proliferation
additional_notes: Clinically approved for FLT3‑mutant AML; active in FLT3‑ITD models (e.g., MOLM‑13). No assay concentrations given in source text.

mechanism: Selective FLT3‑ITD tyrosine kinase inhibitor, inhibits FLT3 signaling
additional_notes: Highly FLT3‑ITD selective; preclinical and clinical activity in FLT3‑ITD AML. No assay concentrations given in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases including FLT3, reducing proliferation and survival signaling
additional_notes: Approved in combination therapy for FLT3‑mutant AML. No assay concentrations given in source text.

mechanism: Type I FLT3 inhibitor active against FLT3‑ITD and many resistance mutants
additional_notes: Reported activity against FLT3 resistance mutants; relevant for FLT3‑driven AML. No assay concentrations given in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases (RAF, VEGFR, PDGFR, FLT3 among others) to reduce proliferation
additional_notes: Used off‑label and in preclinical FLT3‑ITD models. No assay concentrations given in source text.

mechanism: Selective inhibitor of BCL‑2, promoting apoptosis in BCL‑2–dependent cells
additional_notes: Substantial single‑agent and combination activity in AML; synergizes with FLT3 inhibitors and hypomethylating agents. No assay concentrations given in source text.

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA/RNA and inhibits DNA methyltransferases, leading to hypomethylation and altered gene expression
additional_notes: Standard AML therapy used in combinations. No assay concentrations given in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits DNA methyltransferases after incorporation into DNA, causing DNA hypomethylation and cytotoxicity
additional_notes: Widely used in AML therapy and combinations. No assay concentrations given in source text.

mechanism: Disrupts menin‑MLL (KMT2A) interaction, impairing MLL‑fusion driven transcriptional programs
additional_notes: Example menin inhibitor (SNDX‑5613 / revumenib) relevant for MLL‑rearranged AML such as MOLM‑13. No assay concentrations given in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits BET bromodomain proteins (e.g., BRD4), disrupting oncogenic transcriptional programs
additional_notes: Representative of BET inhibitors (OTX015 / JQ1 family) with activity in AML. No assay concentrations given in source text.

mechanism: Reversibly inhibits the 26S proteasome, leading to accumulation of misfolded proteins and apoptosis
additional_notes: Reported preclinical activity and synergistic combinations in AML. No assay concentrations given in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits CDKs (notably CDK9), causing transcriptional downregulation of short‑lived survival proteins
additional_notes: Also known as flavopiridol; clinical and preclinical AML activity. No assay concentrations given in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases (HDACs), altering chromatin and gene expression to induce apoptosis
additional_notes: Has preclinical AML activity and clinical combination studies with HMAs. No assay concentrations given in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits HDACs, increasing histone acetylation and affecting transcriptional programs
additional_notes: Also called SAHA; preclinical AML data and combination potential. No assay concentrations given in source text.

mechanism: Selectively inhibits CDK4/6, inducing G1 cell‑cycle arrest
additional_notes: Selective activity in subsets of AML; potential for combinations. No assay concentrations given in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 in the MAPK pathway, reducing MAPK‑driven proliferation/survival signaling
additional_notes: Useful when MAPK pathway activation contributes to AML survival. No assay concentrations given in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits mTORC1, suppressing mTOR‑dependent growth and survival signaling
additional_notes: Preclinical AML activity and combination potential. No assay concentrations given in source text.

mechanism: Selectively inhibits PI3Kδ isoform, modulating PI3K/AKT signaling
additional_notes: Limited single‑agent activity in AML but may potentiate other agents. No assay concentrations given in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits mutant IDH1, reducing oncometabolite (2‑HG) production and promoting differentiation
additional_notes: Clinically active in IDH1‑mutant AML; relevant only for IDH1‑mutant cases. No assay concentrations given in source text.

mechanism: Anti‑CD33 antibody linked to calicheamicin; binds CD33 on blasts, internalizes and releases cytotoxic payload
additional_notes: Approved for CD33+ AML; biologic ADC rather than small molecule. No assay concentrations given in source text.
